mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates for equity valuations, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns exceed 60% of triggered instances despite rising rates.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances, or signal loses predictive power when unemployment is below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects strengthening economic output that generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during recovery phases.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative returns in >60% of triggered instances during recovery periods, or loses predictive power when growth exceeds 3% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 200 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of instances when CPI exceeds 200, or signal fails to predict direction during high inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm robust labor market expansion that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Combined labor strength signals yield negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product levels reflect stronger aggregate economic activity that translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows, or positive returns occur in <50% of high GDP observations.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0 percent signals contracting manufacturing output that immediately triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or signal loses predictive power when declines are below 0.5 percent.
